title: Subsistence Mining at Kabwe's Black Mountain | Pulitzer Center
impact_evidence: Environmental | Land, Soil & Geology | Tailings generation and management -> "From 1904 until 1994 when the mine was shuttered, an estimated 6.5 million metric tonnes of mining slag accumulated here." | "More than 6 million metric tons of lead slag form Black Mountain, a 30-meter pile of toxic lead waste" || Environmental | Land, Soil & Geology | Surface ground instability and subsidence -> "He explained that the slag is unstable and that there are no materials to shore up the deep tunnels." | "Tunneling through the soft tailings is dangerous. The smelter produces manganese and spews dense plumes of toxic smoke over the Kabwe area." || Environmental | Air & Climate | Particulate matter emissions -> "The smelter produces manganese and spews dense plumes of toxic smoke over the Kabwe area." || Social | Health, Safety & Well-being | Community exposure to dust, noise, pollution -> "unchecked mining, smelting, and unregulated dumping of mining slag has poisoned untold thousands and turned Kabwe into one of the most polluted cities on the planet and certainly Africa's most toxic town." || Social | Health, Safety & Well-being | Worker health and safety risks -> "dozens of scavengers—loosely organized gangs of men and young boys—use picks and shovels to burrow into the unstable slag to find bits of lead and high-grade ore" | "Injuries are common at Black Mountain.
